name: Utah SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
description: Utah's food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to purchase groceries. Administered by the Utah Department of Workforce Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: Utah Medicaid
description: Utah's Medicaid program providing health coverage for eligible low-income residents. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, mental health, and dental services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and individuals with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL under Medicaid expansion; children up to 200% FPL

name: Utah Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary wage replacement benefits to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by the Utah Department of Workforce Services.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage/work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: Utah Housing Corporation (UHC)
description: Provides affordable housing resources including down payment assistance, first-time homebuyer programs, and rental housing development for Utah residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; income and purchase price limits apply

name: Utah Legal Services
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Utahns in matters including housing, family law, consumer issues, public benefits, and immigration.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals at or below 125% of the federal poverty level
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 125% FPL or below

name: Utah Department of Workforce Services - Employment Centers
description: Utah's workforce development system offering job search assistance, training programs, resume help, career counseling, and access to multiple assistance programs.
eligibilitySummary: All Utah residents seeking employment or career development services

name: Utah HEAT (Home Energy Assistance Target - LIHEAP)
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income households pay heating bills. Utah's LIHEAP program administered by the Department of Workforce Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households with home energy costs, prioritizing elderly, disabled, and young children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% FPL

name: Utah 211
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting Utahns with local health and human services resources including food, housing, utilities, and crisis support.
eligibilitySummary: All Utah residents seeking assistance or referrals
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter

name: Utah Food Bank
description: Feeding America affiliate serving all 29 Utah counties. Distributes food through a network of 360+ partner agencies including food pantries, shelters, and community programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in Utah

name: Utah Department of Veterans and Military Affairs
description: Assists Utah veterans and their families in obtaining federal and state benefits including healthcare, disability compensation, education, and employment services.
eligibilitySummary: Veterans, active duty service members, and their dependents residing in Utah

name: Utah Crisis Line
description: Statewide crisis line providing immediate support for mental health emergencies, suicidal thoughts, and emotional distress for Utah residents.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Utah experiencing a mental health crisis

name: Utah Child Care Assistance
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income families pay for childcare so parents can work or attend school or training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children who need childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 85% SMI

name: Utah FEP (Family Employment Program / TANF)
description: Utah's TANF cash assistance program providing temporary financial help and employment services to low-income families with children to achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children who participate in employment activities
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income thresholds; varies by family size

name: Utah WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children supplemental nutrition program providing healthy foods, nutrition education, and breastfeeding support for eligible participants.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children up to age 5
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% FPL

name: Utah Transit Authority (UTA)
description: Public transit system serving the Wasatch Front including Salt Lake City, Provo, and Ogden with bus, TRAX light rail, FrontRunner commuter rail, and paratransit.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders along the Wasatch Front; reduced fares for seniors, disabled, and low-income riders
incomeLimitNotes: Reduced fare available for households at or below 150% FPL
hours: Varies by route and service type

name: Utah Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes to reduce energy costs. Includes insulation, air sealing, and heating system upgrades.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% FPL

name: Utah Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides vocational rehabilitation services for Utahns with disabilities including job training, placement assistance, assistive technology, and career counseling.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Housing Authority of Salt Lake City
description: Provides affordable public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in Salt Lake City.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in Salt Lake City
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 80% AMI for admission

name: Utah Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, shelter referrals, and support for victims of domestic violence through the Utah Domestic Violence Coalition.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or affected by domestic violence in Utah
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter

name: Utah Scholars Pathway / Financial Aid
description: State financial aid and scholarship programs including the Regents' Scholarship and Utah Promise for students attending in-state colleges and universities.
eligibilitySummary: Utah residents pursuing post-secondary education at eligible in-state institutions
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; some need-based, some merit-based

name: Utah Adult Education
description: Provides free GED preparation, adult basic education, English as a Second Language, and workforce literacy programs at locations across Utah.
eligibilitySummary: Adults who need basic education, GED preparation, or English language instruction

name: Community Action Partnership of Utah
description: Network of community action agencies providing anti-poverty services including emergency assistance, housing, energy, and workforce development programs across Utah.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families throughout Utah
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally 125-200% FPL

name: myCase Benefits Portal (Utah)
description: Utah's online portal for applying to and managing public assistance programs including SNAP, Medicaid, childcare, and cash assistance through Workforce Services.
eligibilitySummary: All Utah residents can use this portal to apply for multiple assistance programs
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program

name: Road Home (Salt Lake City Homeless Services)
description: Provides homeless services in the Salt Lake City area including emergency shelter, case management, housing placement, and supportive services for individuals and families.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness in the Salt Lake City area
hours: 24/7 for emergency shelter; office hours Monday-Friday

name: Utah Head Start Association
description: Federally funded early childhood education program promoting school readiness for children from birth to age 5 in low-income Utah families.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children birth to age 5 at or below federal poverty guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: 100% FPL or below; foster and homeless children automatically eligible

name: Utah Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounts on monthly telephone or broadband service for qualifying low-income Utah households to maintain access to essential communications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal assistance program

name: Catholic Community Services of Utah
description: Provides comprehensive social services including refugee resettlement, food pantries, homeless services, immigration assistance, and community support programs.
eligibilitySummary: All individuals and families in need regardless of faith
languages: English, Spanish, and multiple refugee languages

name: Utah Rent Relief Program
description: Provides emergency rental and utility assistance to Utah tenants and landlords to prevent eviction and maintain housing stability during financial hardship.
eligibilitySummary: Renters who have experienced financial hardship and are at risk of eviction or housing instability
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 80% AMI

name: And Justice for All (Utah)
description: Coalition of Utah legal aid organizations providing free legal help finder tool and coordinating pro bono legal services for low-income residents across the state.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals seeking free legal assistance in Utah
incomeLimitNotes: Generally 125-200% FPL depending on program

name: Provo Housing Authority
description: Provides affordable public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Provo area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Provo area
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 80% AMI for admission
